series.title: [PATCH] target: convert TABS indentation to spaces for consistency

review.summary: The patch appears semantically neutral, but it is a broad whitespace-only cleanup over unrelated target headers with little technical benefit. In QEMU this kind of churn is usually discouraged unless it is tightly scoped, automated, or bundled with nearby functional work.

stages.A: Patch scope is a mechanical formatting change only: 5 target headers, 347 insertions and 347 deletions, no intended behavioral change.
stages.B: No functional regression is evident from the diff itself; changes are spacing/alignment in macros, comments, and struct member indentation.
stages.C: Primary concern is process and maintainability: the patch creates large blame/history churn across unrelated architecture code for cosmetic reasons only.
stages.D: Reported validation is weak for a treewide-style cleanup: git-am succeeded and configure passed, but no build or runtime coverage was provided.
stages.E: Recommendation: do not take this as-is. Either drop it, or restrict cleanup to code being touched for a functional change; if maintainers still want reformatting, split by subsystem and justify the mechanical conversion approach.

findings.title: Whitespace-only churn across stable target headers without functional payoff
findings.description: This patch rewrites large tables of architecture constants and packed-struct declarations purely to replace tabs with spaces. The resulting semantic value is effectively zero, while the cost is permanent blame/history noise across stable headers. In QEMU, that tradeoff is usually not worth taking unless the cleanup is incidental to a real code change or explicitly requested as a scripted treewide conversion.
findings.suggestion: Drop the standalone whitespace-only cleanup. If you want these lines reformatted, do it opportunistically in patches that already modify the same code, or submit a clearly justified scripted cleanup with maintainer buy-in.
findings.confidence_reason: The diff is entirely cosmetic, touches many unrelated definitions, and does not change generated code or interfaces.

findings.title: Single patch mixes unrelated target subsystems, increasing review and merge friction
findings.description: The patch bundles alpha, i386, microblaze, and sparc headers into one cosmetic change. Even for trivial cleanups, combining unrelated targets broadens the reviewer set, increases conflict risk, and makes it harder for maintainers to take only the part relevant to their subsystem.
findings.suggestion: If this cleanup is pursued at all, split it into per-subsystem patches so the relevant maintainers can review and merge independently.
findings.confidence_reason: The patch spans multiple unrelated target directories, which is directly visible in the diff header and is a standard maintainability concern.
